narrative:
After a cold start to the day with frost in the valleys, skies are mostly sunny with a noticeably milder afternoon. The warm-up continues without delay for Wednesday through Friday. Skies will be mostly sunny through the period, with a few extra clouds for Wednesday afternoon. The next opportunity for sorely-needed rainfall comes Saturday afternoon.

This week's Monday Message addresses the temperature roller-coaster, drought status, and improving rain prospects for next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 836 photos from 208 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges meet to start the process of selecting around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started by Thursday.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 836 photos from 208 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges meet to start the process of selecting around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started by Thursday.

Forecast Discussion

Canadian high pressure will be shifting to the Delmarva coast today, and that will offer light, southerly breezes to usher in a milder afternoon across western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia.

A cold front sags into the Mason-Dixon area on Wednesday, and may bring showers as far south as southern West Virginia. Here locally, we remain dry, but there will be some extra clouds around during Wednesday afternoon as temperatures continue their ascent.

Thursday and Friday will be wonderful with sunshine and warm temperatures, perfectly suitable for outdoor activities.

This weekend, a cold front will approach on Saturday with scattered showers and thundershowers during the afternoon. The front slips south and east on Sunday promoting a dry and slightly cooler day.

Early next week, the front may reactivate as low pressure develops over the Plains. This may provide another most welcome opportunity for rainfall focused on the Monday PM-Tuesday time frame. Temperatures will be cooler, but really just closer to normal.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
